Details: Based on one study, removal of inorganic mercury from water by precipitative softening can be effective (30 to 70 percent removal).  In the same study, precipitative softening did not effectively remove organic mercury from the water (0 percent removal).  Precipitative softening is considered a Best Available Technology by the USEPA for removing mercury.  Removal of mercury by lime softening depends on pH, mercury species, lime doses, and the presence of co-occuring solutes, such as manganese and iron.  A softening pH between 9 and 11 appears to be ideal for mercury removal.
